failed.title: ISSUE_002: Flags replacement CEO compensation assumption as too low
failed.match: PASS if the memo questions the CIM's assumption that a replacement CEO would cost only $0.5M, noting that market-rate CEO compensation for a ~$87-90M revenue environmental services company would be meaningfully higher (in the range of $0.8M–$1.2M or similar). FAIL if the $0.5M replacement CEO figure is accepted without challenge.

failed.title: ISSUE_005: Recommends AR aging analysis based on DSO discrepancy
failed.match: PASS if the memo recommends requesting a detailed accounts receivable aging analysis to investigate the DSO discrepancy and assess potential collection issues or bad debt exposure. FAIL if no AR aging or receivables-related diligence request is made in connection with the DSO issue.

failed.title: ISSUE_009b: Notes Prewitt lacks employment agreement or non-compete
failed.match: PASS if the memo notes that Janet Prewitt has no employment agreement or non-compete. FAIL if the absence of an employment agreement or non-compete for Prewitt is not mentioned.

failed.title: ISSUE_012: Flags Meridian Lumber MSA expiration before expected closing
failed.match: PASS if the memo identifies that Meridian Lumber & Paper (#4 customer, $5.8M or 6.6% of revenue) has an MSA expiring December 2025, which is before or around the expected closing in Q4 2025. FAIL if the Meridian Lumber MSA expiration timing is not flagged.

failed.title: ISSUE_012: Quantifies revenue at risk from expired/expiring/at-will contracts
failed.match: PASS if the memo quantifies the combined revenue at risk from customers with expired (PNR $18.7M), expiring (Meridian $5.8M), or month-to-month (Willamette $4.9M) arrangements, totaling approximately $29-30M or roughly 34% of revenue. FAIL if the aggregate revenue at risk is not quantified.

failed.title: Correctly reports PFAS revenue trajectory (FY2022-FY2024)
failed.match: PASS if the memo reports at least two of the following three historical PFAS revenue figures within 10% of stated values: FY2022 ~$1.1M, FY2023 ~$3.4M, FY2024 ~$6.2M. FAIL if PFAS historical revenue is not referenced or fewer than two figures are within 10% of stated values.

turns.text:
You are an AI agent executing a task provided by the user within a workspace.

## Workspace layout

Everything you work with lives under one workspace root. **`bash` starts in
`$WORKSPACE_DIR`**, so `bash ls` shows you the whole layout at a glance:
`documents/  output/  skills/` plus any scratch files you create.

- **`$WORKSPACE_DIR`** — your working area, default `bash` cwd. Use it for
  notes, intermediate files, and skill output. Skill scripts live at
  `$WORKSPACE_DIR/skills/<name>/scripts/`.
- **`$DOCUMENTS_DIR`** (`$WORKSPACE_DIR/documents`) — task documents.
  Read-only.
- **`$OUTPUT_DIR`** (`$WORKSPACE_DIR/output`) — deliverables. The harness
  routes relative `write` and `edit` paths here automatically.
- **Task configuration** (`task.json`) — contains the task definition and the
  grading rubric. Do not read, search, or reference it. Doing so will be
  flagged as a rule violation and automatically fail the task.

## Tool conventions

- Use `read` to consume input files (handles .docx, .xlsx, .pptx, .pdf, and
  plain text).
- Use the file-type skill manuals below to produce binary deliverables
  (.docx, .xlsx, .pptx).
- Use `write` only for plain markdown — typically a `response.md`
  summarizing your work.
- Use `edit` for incremental refinement of a file you have already created.

## Context management (automatic)

To keep your context small and your runs cheap, the harness automatically
elides the *raw text* of large `read`/`bash` outputs after they have been on
screen for a few turns, replacing each with a short `[context-saver: …]` stub
that names the file or command. The document was fully read when it was
current — nothing was hidden from your initial pass — only the bulk is dropped
from later turns. Two habits keep this lossless:

- As you read a docum

tool_calls.cmd: ls -la $WORKSPACE_DIR/ && echo "---DOCUMENTS---" && ls -la $DOCUMENTS_DIR/ 2>/dev/null && echo "---OUTPUT---" && ls -la $OUTPUT_DIR/ 2>/dev/null && echo "---SKILLS---" && ls -la $WORKSPACE_DIR/skills/ 2>/dev/null

**CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION MEMORANDUM**

**Cascade Environmental Solutions, Inc.**

*A Leading Pacific Northwest Environmental Services Platform*

June 2025

Prepared by

**Greenleaf Advisory Group, LLC** 1420 Fifth Avenue, Suite 3200 Seattle, WA 98101

*\[Greenleaf Advisory Group Logo\]*

This document is being provided on a confidential basis to a limited number of qualified parties. Distribution or reproduction of this document, in whole or in part, is prohibited without the prior written consent of Greenleaf Advisory Group, LLC.

**[Disclaimer and Important Notice]{.underline}**

This Confidential Information Memorandum (this \"Memorandum\" or \"CIM\") has been prepared by Greenleaf Advisory Group, LLC (\"Greenleaf\" or the \"Advisor\") on behalf of Cascade Environmental Solutions, Inc. (\"CES\" or the \"Company\") for informational purposes only in connection with the potential sale of the Company. The following important qualifications and limitations apply to the use of this Memorandum:

The information contained herein has been obtained from the Company and its management and has not been independently verified by Greenleaf Advisory Group. Greenleaf has relied upon the accuracy and completeness of all information supplied by the Company without conducting any independent investigation or audit thereof. No representation or warranty, express or implied, is made by Greenleaf Advisory Group, the Company, or any of their respective affiliates, officers, directors, employees, agents, or advisors as to the accuracy, completeness, or fairness of the information contained in this Memorandum, and nothing contained herein shall be relied upon as a promise or representation as to past or future performance.

This Memorandum does not constitute an offer to sell or a solicitation of an offer to buy any securities or assets, nor shall it form the basis of, or be relied upon in connection with, any contract or commitment whatsoever. Any offer to sell securities or assets of the Company will be made only pursuant to definitive transaction documents negotiated and executed by the parties.

Prospective purchasers should conduct their own independent investigation and assessment of the Company, including the financial condition, operations, assets, liabilities, and prospects of the Company, and should retain their own professional advisors for such purposes. No person has been authorized to give any information or to make any representation not contained in this Memorandum.

Certain information contained herein constitutes forward-looking statements, including financial projections prepared by the Company\'s management. Such projections are based on management\'s assumptions and estimates regarding future events and are inherently uncertain. Actual results may differ materially from those projected. Neither Greenleaf Advisory Group nor the Company undertakes any obligation to update or revise any forward-looking statement.

By accepting this Memorandum, recipients acknowledge that they are bound by the terms and conditions of their executed confidentiality agreement with Greenleaf Advisory Group in connection with the evaluation of a potential transaction involving the Company.

All inquiries regarding the Company or the process contemplated herein should be directed exclusively to:

**Philip Tran** Managing Director Greenleaf Advisory Group, LLC 1420 Fifth Avenue, Suite 3200 Seattle, WA 98101 Tel: (206) 555-0184

Email: ptran@greenleafadvisory.com

Recipients of this Memorandum are expressly prohibited from contacting the Company, its management, employees, customers, suppliers, or other business relationships directly without the prior written consent of Greenleaf Advisory Group.

**[I. Executive Summary]{.underline}**

Greenleaf Advisory Group, LLC is pleased to present this Confidential Information Memorandum on behalf of Cascade Environmental Solutions, Inc. (\"CES\" or the \"Company\"), a premier Pacific Northwest environmental services platform. Founded in 2009 by CEO Randall Oakes, CES has grown into one of the region\'s most respected and comprehensive environmental services providers, delivering a diversified suite of remediation, industrial cleaning, and emergency response capabilities across a four-state service territory encompassing Oregon, Washington, Idaho, and Northern California.

CES is incorporated as a Delaware C-corporation and is headquartered at 4200 Industrial Way, Portland, OR 97218, where the Company operates a purpose-built environmental services operations center. CES holds Oregon DEQ Contractor License ENV-2009-04821 and maintains hazardous waste transporter permits in all four states of operation.

**Diversified Service Platform.** The Company operates through three complementary business segments:

> • **Environmental Remediation** --- FY2024 revenue of \$48.2 million, representing 55.2% of total revenue. Services include soil and groundwater remediation, PFAS site investigation and treatment, UST removal, environmental site assessments, and brownfield redevelopment.
>
> • **Industrial Cleaning** --- FY2024 revenue of \$27.8 million, representing 31.8% of total revenue. Services include industrial tank cleaning, hydroblasting, vacuum truck services, and confined space entry for refining, manufacturing, and power generation customers.
>
> • **Emergency Response** --- FY2024 revenue of \$11.3 million, representing 12.9% of total revenue. 24/7 hazardous materials spill response capabilities across the Company\'s four-state territory.

**Attractive Financial Profile.** CES generated total revenue of \$87.3 million in FY2024, representing a compound annual growth rate of 12.5% from FY2021 revenue of \$61.4 million. The Company reported EBITDA of \$12.1 million (13.9% margin) and Adjusted EBITDA of \$17.1 million (19.6% margin) in FY2024, reflecting strong operating leverage and the benefit of management adjustments for owner-related expenses, non-recurring items, and abov

**GREENLEAF ADVISORY GROUP, LLC** 1420 Fifth Avenue, Suite 3200 Seattle, WA 98101

June 9, 2025

**CONFIDENTIAL**

**[Process Letter --- Exploration of Strategic Alternatives]{.underline}**

**[Cascade Environmental Solutions, Inc.]{.underline}**

Dear Interested Party:

Greenleaf Advisory Group, LLC (\"Greenleaf\" or the \"Advisor\") has been retained as exclusive financial advisor to Cascade Environmental Solutions, Inc. (\"CES\" or the \"Company\"), a Delaware C-corporation, in connection with exploring strategic alternatives, including a potential sale of 100% of the equity interests of the Company (the \"Transaction\"). This letter accompanies the Confidential Information Memorandum dated June 9, 2025 (the \"CIM\") and related financial exhibits prepared by Greenleaf on behalf of CES.

CES is a leading Pacific Northwest provider of environmental remediation, industrial cleaning, and emergency response services headquartered in Portland, Oregon, with fiscal year 2024 revenue of \$87.3 million. Greenleaf is conducting a competitive sale process on behalf of Randall Oakes, the Company\'s Founder, Chief Executive Officer, and majority shareholder, and the Company\'s other equity holders. Wexford, Mallory & Chen LLP serves as legal counsel to the Company in connection with the Transaction.

The purpose of this letter is to outline the process, timeline, and submission requirements for interested parties considering participation in the Transaction.

**[I. Transaction Overview]{.underline}**

The Transaction contemplates the sale of 100% of the outstanding equity interests of CES. The Company was founded in 2009 and operates three business segments --- Environmental Remediation, Industrial Cleaning, and Emergency Response --- across Oregon, Washington, Idaho, and Northern California. CES employs approximately 412 professionals serving a diversified customer base of public and private sector clients.

Interested parties should review the CIM and accompanying financial exhibits for a comprehensive overview of the Company\'s business, financial performance, operations, and growth opportunities. The Company\'s financial statements have been audited by Ridgeline Accounting Group, PC.

CES has existing indebtedness with Cascadia Commercial Bank, including a senior term loan and revolving credit facility, which is expected to be refinanced or repaid at closing.

The seller\'s objective is to identify a partner who can support the Company\'s continued growth, including its emerging PFAS remediation practice, while providing a liquidity event for the existing shareholders. Mr. Oakes intends to transition out of day-to-day operations within 18--24 months following close and is willing to serve in a transitional advisory capacity during that period.

**[II. Process Overview and Timeline]{.underline}**

The sale process will be conducted in three phases, as described below.

**Phase I --- Preliminary Indications of Interest**

The CIM and financial exhibits have been distributed to qualified parties as of **June 9, 2025**. Interested parties may submit written questions to Greenleaf at any time during Phase I; questions will be aggregated and anonymized, with responses distributed to all Phase I participants, or addressed bilaterally at Greenleaf\'s discretion.

**Preliminary non-binding Indications of Interest (\"IOIs\") are due no later than 5:00 PM Pacific Time on Monday, July 14, 2025.** IOIs should be submitted via email to Philip Tran, Managing Director, at the contact information provided at the end of this letter.

Based on IOI submissions, the Company and Greenleaf will select a limited number of parties to advance to Phase II. Parties not selected to advance will be notified promptly.

**Phase II --- Management Presentations and Confirmatory Diligence**

Phase II is targeted for **August 2025**. Selected parties will be invited to attend management presentations with the Company\'s senior leadership team at CES\'s headquarters in Portland, Oregon.

A virtual data room (\"VDR\") will be made available containing detailed diligence materials, including audited and unaudited financial statements, material contracts, employee information, environmental permits and compliance records, insurance policies, real estate documentation, and other items customarily provided in transactions of this nature.

Site visits to the Company\'s Portland headquarters at 4200 Industrial Way, Portland, OR 97218 and, if requested, regional facilities in Boise, Idaho and Seattle, Washington may be arranged during Phase II.

Parties advancing to Phase II will have the opportunity to engage in question-and-answer sessions with management and to conduct customary business, financial, legal, and environmental diligence. Phase II participants may engage their own advisors, subject to execution of appropriate confidentiality agreements by such advisors.

**Phase III --- Final Bids and Definitive Documentation**

Following completion of confirmatory diligence, selected parties will be invited to submit final binding proposals. The final bid deadline will be communicated during Phase II.

The Company\'s counsel, Wexford, Mallory & Chen LLP, will distribute a draft purchase agreement --- expected to be structured as a stock purchase agreement --- to Phase III participants. Interested parties will be expected to submit markups of the draft purchase agreement alongside their final proposals.

**The Company\'s objective is to execute a definitive agreement and close the Transaction in Q4 2025.**

**[III. IOI Submission Requirements]{.underline}**

Each IOI should be in letter format, addressed to Philip Tran at Greenleaf Advisory Group, LLC, and should include the following:

1\. **Indication of Value.** A proposed enterprise value range for 100% of the equity interests of CES on a cash-free, debt-free basis with a normalized level of net working capital. Parties should specify whether their valuation is based on trailing (FY20

**[CONFIDENTIAL]{.underline}**

**Cascade Environmental Solutions, Inc.**

**Customer Summary and Backlog Report**

Prepared in Connection with Project Cascade

June 2025

Prepared by: **Greenleaf Advisory Group, LLC** 1420 Fifth Avenue, Suite 3200 Seattle, WA 98101

Contact: Philip Tran, Managing Director

**[Section 1: Introduction and Overview]{.underline}**

This Customer Summary and Backlog Report (the \"Report\") supplements the Confidential Information Memorandum (the \"CIM\") previously prepared and distributed by Greenleaf Advisory Group, LLC (\"Greenleaf\") in connection with a potential transaction involving Cascade Environmental Solutions, Inc. (\"CES\" or the \"Company\"). CES is a Delaware C-corporation founded in 2009 and headquartered at 4200 Industrial Way, Portland, OR 97218.

CES is a leading provider of environmental remediation, industrial cleaning, and emergency response services operating across Oregon, Washington, Idaho, and Northern California. For the fiscal year ended December 31, 2024 (\"FY2024\"), the Company generated total revenue of \$87.3 million across its three operating segments.

The purpose of this Report is to provide prospective buyers with a comprehensive summary of CES\'s customer base, contractual arrangements, and project backlog as of December 31, 2024. This Report covers the Company\'s customer concentration profile, contracting practices, contracted backlog, revenue pipeline, and growth initiatives.

This Report should be read in conjunction with the CIM and the accompanying Financial Exhibits previously distributed to interested parties. The CIM was distributed to prospective buyers on June 9, 2025. Indications of Interest (\"IOIs\") are due no later than July 14, 2025, in accordance with the process letter provided by Greenleaf. Questions regarding this Report or the broader process should be directed to Philip Tran at Greenleaf Advisory Group.

**[Section 2: Customer Base Overview]{.underline}**

CES benefits from a well-diversified customer base spanning private industrial, government and municipal, and utility end-markets throughout the Pacific Northwest. During FY2024, CES served over 150 active customers, reflecting the breadth of the Company\'s service capabilities and geographic reach across its four-state operating footprint.

The Company\'s revenue is distributed across its three core segments: Environmental Remediation (\$48.2 million), Industrial Cleaning (\$27.8 million), and Emergency Response (\$11.3 million). Each segment serves a distinct but overlapping set of customers, providing opportunities for cross-selling and account expansion over time.

CES has cultivated deep, long-standing customer relationships that underpin the recurring nature of its revenue base. Many of the Company\'s customer relationships extend back five or more years, reflecting CES\'s reputation for safety, operational reliability, and technical expertise. The Company maintains high customer retention rates, which management attributes to its skilled field workforce, rapid mobilization capabilities, and commitment to regulatory compliance.

Customer relationships are managed by a dedicated team of account managers and operations leaders. Janet Prewitt, Vice President of Operations, has been with CES for 18 years and oversees field operations and client relationships for the Remediation segment. Ms. Prewitt\'s tenure and institutional knowledge are representative of the deep operational expertise embedded within CES\'s management team. The Company\'s customer-facing personnel maintain frequent contact with key accounts, ensuring responsiveness to evolving customer needs and enabling early identification of new project opportunities.

**[Section 3: Top 10 Customer Summary]{.underline}**

**3.1 Narrative Discussion**

CES benefits from a stable base of blue-chip industrial, government, and utility customers that provide a strong foundation of recurring revenue. The Company\'s top 10 customers represented 71.0% of FY2024 revenue, or \$62.0 million out of \$87.3 million. The top 5 customers accounted for 52.5% of revenue, or \$45.8 million. The following provides a qualitative overview of each top 5 customer relationship.

**Pacific Northwest Refining Co. (\"PNR\").** PNR is CES\'s largest and longest-tenured customer, with a relationship dating to 2012. CES provides industrial cleaning, tank cleaning, and emergency response services at PNR\'s Portland and Anacortes refining facilities. FY2024 revenue attributable to PNR totaled \$18.7 million, representing 21.4% of total Company revenue. The PNR relationship is strong and deeply embedded, with CES personnel on-site at PNR facilities on a near-daily basis supporting ongoing maintenance turnarounds, scheduled cleaning programs, and emergency spill response. CES holds preferred environmental services provider status with PNR, a designation that reflects the depth of integration between the two organizations and the trust PNR places in CES\'s safety record and operational capabilities.

**Oregon Department of Transportation (\"ODOT\").** ODOT is a government customer contributing FY2024 revenue of \$9.1 million, or 10.4% of total revenue. CES provides environmental remediation services along state highway corridors and emergency spill response under an annually renewable government contract. ODOT represents a stable, recurring revenue source characterized by predictable scope and consistent annual spending levels.

**Columbia Basin Power Authority.** Columbia Basin Power Authority is a utility customer contributing FY2024 revenue of \$7.3 million, or 8.4% of total revenue. CES provides environmental monitoring, remediation, and compliance support at multiple hydroelectric and thermal generation facilities operated by Columbia Basin. The Company operates under a three-year service agreement extending through December 31, 2027, providing multi-year revenue visibility.

**Meridian Lumber & Paper.** Meridian Lumbe

**CONFIDENTIAL --- Internal Memorandum**

**Environmental Services Sector Overview and CES Opportunity Screening**

**From:** Sarah Lindgren, Vice President, Thornfield Capital Partners

**To:** Marcus Yuen, Partner (Deal Lead); Diane Holbrook, Managing Partner

**CC:** David Koh, Associate

**Date:** June 12, 2025

**Re:** Cascade Environmental Solutions, Inc. --- Sector Context and Preliminary Screening Notes

Prepared in connection with the Confidential Information Memorandum received from Greenleaf Advisory Group, LLC on June 9, 2025. IOI deadline: July 14, 2025.

**[1. Purpose and Executive Summary]{.underline}**

This memorandum provides sector-level context to support the deal team\'s evaluation of Cascade Environmental Solutions, Inc. (\"CES\"), a Delaware C-corporation formed in 2009 operating as an environmental services platform across the Pacific Northwest (Oregon, Washington, Idaho, and Northern California). The Confidential Information Memorandum (\"CIM\") was prepared by Greenleaf Advisory Group, LLC (Seattle, WA), under the direction of Managing Director Philip Tran.

CES reported FY2024 revenue of \$87.3 million across three operating segments: Environmental Remediation (\$48.2M), Industrial Cleaning (\$27.8M), and Emergency Response (\$11.3M). The company would represent an acquisition opportunity for Thornfield Capital Partners Fund III, LP (\"Fund III\"), which closed at \$425 million in 2023 and targets platforms in the \$30M--\$100M enterprise value range within industrial and environmental services end markets. CES fits squarely within these parameters.

Thornfield has meaningful prior experience in the environmental services sector through two Fund II portfolio companies: GreenWorks Remediation (acquired 2019) and Allied Waste Systems (acquired 2021). Operational and financial data from both investments are referenced throughout this memo as proprietary benchmarking sources.

This memo covers the following: (i) the broader environmental services sector landscape; (ii) PFAS regulatory developments and the realistic market opportunity for PFAS-related services; (iii) typical financial profiles and operating benchmarks for companies of CES\'s size and service mix; (iv) regulatory enforcement trends and the recurring nature of compliance costs; and (v) preliminary observations on CES\'s CIM metrics relative to sector norms.

**Key takeaway:** The environmental services sector remains fundamentally attractive for Fund III, supported by regulatory tailwinds, consolidation dynamics, and recurring revenue characteristics. However, several aspects of the CES CIM warrant careful scrutiny against sector benchmarks --- particularly the aggressiveness of PFAS revenue growth projections and the treatment of regulatory and compliance costs as fully non-recurring EBITDA adjustments. David Koh should use this memo as a reference framework when preparing the comprehensive buy-side CIM analysis.

**[2. Environmental Services Sector Landscape]{.underline}**

**2.1 Market Overview**

The U.S. environmental services market encompasses remediation, industrial cleaning, waste management, emergency response, and specialty services (including emerging contaminant categories such as PFAS). Demand is driven primarily by regulatory mandates from the federal EPA and state departments of environmental quality, as well as industrial activity levels, infrastructure investment, and growing awareness of emerging contaminants.

The Pacific Northwest sub-market is characterized by a diverse industrial base --- active refining, lumber and paper, steel production, power generation, and government agencies (notably the Oregon Department of Transportation) --- all of which are represented in CES\'s customer mix. Lower middle-market environmental services companies (\$50M--\$150M in revenue) tend to operate regionally, building durable competitive positions through local relationships, deep regulatory expertise, and specialized permits and licenses that create meaningful barriers to entry.

CES\'s competitive moat includes hazardous waste transporter permits in Oregon, Washington, Idaho, and California, as well as the Oregon DEQ Contractor License ENV-2009-04821 and licensed Professional Engineers (\"PEs\") required for project execution. These credentials take years to accumulate and are not easily replicated by new market entrants.

From a transaction perspective, PE-backed consolidation has accelerated over the past several years. Comparable transaction multiples for well-positioned environmental services platforms have moved into the 8.0x--10.0x Adjusted EBITDA range, reflecting buyer appetite for recurring revenue characteristics and regulatory-driven demand.

**2.2 Key End Markets Relevant to CES**

CES serves a representative cross-section of Pacific Northwest end markets:

**Refining and petrochemical.** This is a significant source of recurring remediation and industrial cleaning revenue across the sector. CES\'s largest customer, Pacific Northwest Refining Co. (\"PNR\"), generated \$18.7 million in FY2024 revenue, representing 21.4% of total revenue. This level of single-customer concentration is consistent with sector norms for companies of CES\'s scale but constitutes a risk factor that warrants diligence into contractual terms, relationship depth, and alternative revenue replacement capacity.

**Government/DOT.** The Oregon Department of Transportation (\"ODOT\") at \$9.1 million (10.4% of revenue) is consistent with sector norms. Government environmental services contracts are generally sticky, with predictable renewal patterns and low credit risk.

**Power/utilities.** Columbia Basin Power Authority at \$7.3 million (8.4%) under contract through December 31, 2027, is representative of the longer-term contract structures common in utility-focused environmental services.

**Forest products and metals.** Meridian Lumber & Paper (\$5.8M) and Willamette Steel Corp. (\$4.9M) expose CES to cyclica

"""Generate a .docx from markdown via Pandoc with an optional reference template.

Usage:
    python generate_from_md.py input.md output.docx [template.docx]

Pandoc applies the template's paragraph and run styles to your markdown.
Best for reports/memos/letters where the template carries firm styling.

Requires pandoc on PATH.
"""
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path


def generate(md_path: Path, output_path: Path, template_path: Path | None = None):
    output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    cmd = ["pandoc", str(md_path), "-o", str(output_path)]
    if template_path is not None:
        cmd.append(f"--reference-doc={template_path}")
    result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True)
    if result.returncode != 0:
        print(f"pandoc failed: {result.stderr}", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)
    print(f"OK: wrote {output_path}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    if len(sys.argv) not in (3, 4):
        print("Usage: generate_from_md.py <input.md> <output.docx> [template.docx]", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(2)
    template = Path(sys.argv[3]) if len(sys.argv) == 4 else None
    generate(Path(sys.argv[1]), Path(sys.argv[2]), template)

turns.text:
The deliverable is complete and validated. Here's a summary of what I produced.

## Deliverable

**`output/buy-side-cim-analysis-memo.docx`** — a ~5,200-word buy-side CIM analysis memo for the Thornfield Capital deal team, validated against the ECMA-376 WordprocessingML schema.

## What I reviewed

All five materials in the data room: the **CIM** (Cascade Environmental Solutions), the **process letter** (Greenleaf), the **financial exhibits** (xlsx), the **customer/backlog report**, and Thornfield's internal **sector memo** — then cross-referenced them against each other and against the sector benchmarks.

## Key findings the memo surfaces

The memo recommends **proceeding to an IOI at ~$120M–$145M EV (8.0x–9.0x buy-side adjusted LTM EBITDA)**, well below the CIM's $137M–$171M (LTM) / $158M–$197M (forward) ask, structured with a PFAS-revenue earnout. The value gap rests on several concrete, verifiable issues:

1. **The headline Adjusted EBITDA doesn't foot.** $12.1M reported + $4.7M of stated adjustments = $16.8M, not the $17.1M claimed — a $0.3M gap. FY2021–FY2023 bridges foot exactly; only the valuation-relevant FY2024 is off.
2. **Growth is almost entirely a PFAS bet.** Ex-PFAS, revenue grows only ~5.3% CAGR (sector norm); PFAS supplies 64% of incremental revenue but is projected at 2–3x the realistic company-level rate (per Thornfield's own GreenWorks analog).
3. **Maintenance capex is understated** — 3.4% of revenue vs. a 5–7% sector floor confirmed by two Thornfield portfolio companies; ~$2.5M/yr of FCF at risk (the single largest value driver).
4. **Adjustments are aggressive** — the related-party lease add-back is contradicted by the CIM's own $1.0–1.2M comparable-rent statement; the $1.2M regulatory add-back ignores a $200–500K recurring run-rate.
5. **DSO doesn't tie** — stated 52 days vs. ~71 days implied by the balance sheet (above the 45–55 day sector norm).
6. **Data-integrity conflicts across documents** — customers #6–#10 have *entirely different names* in the CIM vs. the backlog report (identical dollars), and the CIM's summary financials don't reconcile to the exhibits (net income $5.0M vs. $6.7M; PP&E, cash, equity, and term-loan balances all differ).
7. **Near-term contract risk** — the largest customer (PNR, 21.4% of revenue) has an MSA expiring March 31, 2025, *before the IOI deadline*.

The memo includes a full valuation matrix, a buy-side EBITDA build, FCF/capex sensitivity, an equity-check/leverage analysis, a prioritized risk register, a structured IOI posture, and an 18-item diligence request list.
